title: T-006 — site_access_policy_v1 DSL rule + site-scoped RLS variant generator (§8, §11.2 V-MS-08, §14.1)
prompt:
# T-006 — site_access_policy_v1 DSL rule + RLS variant generator

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §8 (DSL rules registered via 02-SET §7.8), §11.2 V-MS-08, §14.1 RLS policy management
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Build the rule-driven RLS variant generator so every operational table tagged `site_scoped` (the 20+ in §9.8) gets a uniform RLS policy without per-table copy-paste DDL. This is the engineering enabler for T-030 (activation migration that applies the variant to all 20 tables).

## Implementation contract
1. Rule registration via 02-SET rules_registry seed migration `packages/db/migrations/0045_site_access_policy_v1_rule.sql`:
   - INSERT INTO rule_definitions (id, name='site_access_policy_v1', version=1, body JSONB) describing the DSL: {policy_template: '(org_id = app.current_org_id()) AND (app.current_site_id() IS NULL OR site_id = app.current_site_id())', applies_to: 'tables_with_site_id_column', migration_lint_severity: 'warn'}.
2. New module `packages/db/src/policies/site-access-policy-v1.ts` exports `buildSiteScopedPolicySql(tableName: string): string` returning the canonical CREATE POLICY DDL using the template above + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
3. Lint helper `packages/db/src/policies/lint-site-scoped.ts` exports `assertTableHasSiteScopedPolicy(client, tableName)` for use in 14-e regression tests + future migrations.
4. Tests: round-trip a generated policy against a freshly migrated `_test_widgets(org_id, site_id, name)` table; verify SELECT under withOrgContext+withSiteContext returns expected rows; lint helper detects missing policy.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0045_site_access_policy_v1_rule.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/policies/site-access-policy-v1.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/policies/lint-site-scoped.ts [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/site-access-policy-v1.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0045 applied, when rule_definitions table is queried, then a row name='site_access_policy_v1' version=1 exists with body JSONB containing policy_template, applies_to, migration_lint_severity keys.
2. Given buildSiteScopedPolicySql('foo'), when output is inspected, then it is a single CREATE POLICY ... ON foo USING(...) WITH CHECK(...) ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY statement, and the USING expression text equals the rule's policy_template exactly.
3. Given a test table _test_widgets(org_id, site_id, name) with the generated policy applied, when withOrgContext({orgId:A}) + withSiteContext({siteId:X}) wraps a SELECT, then only rows matching (org_id=A AND site_id=X) are returned.
4. Given _test_widgets has the policy but FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is not enabled, when assertTableHasSiteScopedPolicy is called, then it throws a typed error `MissingForceRowLevelSecurity`.
5. Given the policy SQL is inspected, when grep'd for current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id'|'app.current_site_id'), then zero matches — only function calls.

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/__tests__/site-access-policy-v1.test.ts asserting all 5 ACs before generator exists.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- site-access-policy-v1'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not hardcode policy SQL into the migration for each table — that defeats D-MS-13 rule-driven uniformity.
- Do not allow the template to omit org_id check — defense in depth (site_id alone is not enough; cross-org contamination via collisions impossible but org_id is also needed for index/index-only-scan correctness).
- Do not skip FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY in generator output — V-MS-08 requires it.
- Do not couple generator to a Drizzle-specific runtime — must be plain SQL string output usable from any migration tool.
- Do not let lint helper false-positive on tables that legitimately stay org_scoped (master tables per §9.8 'Master data' list).

pipeline_inputs.description: Register site_access_policy_v1 DSL rule in 02-SET §7.8 rules registry + build migration generator that emits site-scoped RLS variant (org_id = app.current_org_id() AND (app.current_site_id() IS NULL OR site_id = app.current_site_id())) for any table tagged site_scoped in §9.8.

Do not implement the migration linter in CI (developer-experience improvement).

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Rule is stored in rule_definitions table owned by 02-SET §7.8.
Cross-mod dep to 00-foundation T-125 declared (withOrgContext + app.current_org_id() per F11 rule). withSiteContext composes on top of T-125; all site-scoped tasks must carry this cross-module dep.

Given _test_widgets has policy but FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is not enabled, when assertTableHasSiteScopedPolicy called, then it throws MissingForceRowLevelSecurity.
